news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Biopharma veteran takes her CEO Cue
news.snippet: Not long after the departure of former CEO Usman Azam, a new leader is taking her Cue in the form of Shao-Lee Lin, M.D., Ph.D. Lin steps into Cue’s president and chief exec spot after previously servi

news.title: Banyan BioInnovations puts down roots with $100M, Icon partnership
news.snippet: Banyan BioInnovations is debuting with more than $100 million aimed at developing NewCos around promising clinical assets, plus a partnership with global contract research organization Icon.

news.title: BeOne shelves 5 cancer programs, halts phase 2 rheumatoid arthritis trial
news.snippet: BeOne Medicines has shaken up its early pipeline, dropping five phase 1 cancer programs and stopping a phase 2 arthritis trial based on new data.

news.title: Antibodies that stop measles from stabbing cells could become new treatments for the surging virus, study suggests

news.title: FDA to reconsider shock rejection of cell therapy Ebvallo. Could uniQure be next?
news.snippet: After a surprise rejection in January, the FDA has agreed to reconsider a T-cell therapy based on the same single-arm trial that the agency had previously found problematic. The case could reignite in

news.title: Blackstone invests $250M in Anagram to reduce burden of cystic fibrosis complication
news.snippet: For people living with exocrine pancreatic insufficiency due to cystic fibrosis, the only treatment option is a regimen that can include up to 40 pills a day. With a $250 million investment from Black

news.title: Ascendis axes IL-2 program in retreat from oncology expansion
news.snippet: Ascendis Pharma has ended internal development of its IL-2 candidate, pivoting away from oncology and calling time on an asset it once tipped for accelerated approval.

news.title: Roche shells out up to $1B for PathAI in move to boost artificial intelligence-powered diagnostics
news.snippet: Roche is set to pay out a little more than $1 billion to acquire digital pathology specialist Path AI in a move the pharma giant hopes will bolster the use of artificial intelligence in diagnostic alg

news.title: Entrada's stock craters as DMD data underperform expectations, handing advantage to Novartis
news.snippet: Entrada Therapeutics’ Duchenne muscular dystrophy data have fallen well short of expectations, crashing the biotech’s stock as investors evaluated the fallout from a key readout expected to validate i

news.title: From drug development to M&A, Big Pharmas showcase AI’s ‘measurable impact’
news.snippet: The recent run of first-quarter earnings results marked a shift for Big Pharmas away from AI hype and toward explaining how the technology is being applied in practice.

news.title: Insulet boosts guidance in Q1 update as it grapples with recall saga
news.snippet: Insulet is upping its guidance after a strong first quarter, though ongoing recalls continue to cast a shadow for investors.

news.title: Ipsen scuttles liver disease candidates from $952M Albireo acquisition
news.snippet: Ipsen has set aside the two drug candidates it picked up from a 2023 acquisition of liver disease specialist Albireo, with one of the assets discontinued entirely.

news.title: Bipartisan PRIMATE Act aims to ban monkey imports to US
news.snippet: A new bipartisan bill could end the import of primates for U.S. research, targeting the roughly 20,000 monkeys brought into the country each year for laboratories or their suppliers.

news.title: BioCryst scraps eye candidate to focus on rare diseases
news.snippet: The company announced it has concluded development of the plasma kallikrein inhibitor, which was being studied to treat diabetic macular edema.

news.title: GSK pens $1B deal with China’s SiranBio for oligonucleotide that could reduce abdominal fat
news.snippet: GSK has found another potential way to treat cardiometabolic disease with a modality close to the pharma’s heart—oligonucleotides.

news.title: Angelini finds Catalyst for its US growth ambitions with $4.1B buyout
news.snippet: With an eye on the lucrative U.S. market, Italy’s Angelini Pharma will acquire rare disease specialist Catalyst Pharmaceuticals and its potential blockbuster Firdapse for $4.1 billion.

news.title: The timing is right for psychedelics manufacturer Optimi Health's IPO
news.snippet: Last month, when Optimi Health kicked off an initial public offering of 2.5 million shares, the timing couldn’t have been better for the Vancouver-based manufacturer of psychedelic drugs. With Pr

news.title: Insulet CEO details ‘Scrubs’ device promotion win as it boosts awareness, education drive
news.snippet: Insulet has always been a strong leader in the medtech industry when it comes to its marketing game—and the diabetes device maker isn’t looking to rest on its laurels.

news.title: Alnylam rebuked by FDA over efficacy claims on Amvuttra website
news.snippet: The FDA has hit Alnylam with an untitled letter over the consumer website for Amvuttra, accusing the biotech of presenting open-label data that create a misleading impression of the drug’s effects.

news.title: Sanofi asks to pull Tzield bid from FDA's controversial CNPV program: report
news.snippet: The FDA's Commissioner's National Priority Voucher program has been steeped in controversy since its inception. Now, Sanofi has asked that one of its applications be taken out of the fast-track progra

news.title: FDA rolls out 1-day assessment pilot in bid to refocus inspection resources
news.snippet: In a bid to better deploy its boots-on-the-ground resources, the FDA is rolling out a new pilot program, under which the regulator is leveraging “one-day inspectional assessments” to complement its tr

news.title: Novo CEO cites pricing 'sweet spot' as Wegovy pill debuts with $355M quarter
news.snippet: After several consecutive quarters on the backfoot, Danish drug juggernaut Novo Nordisk is receiving warmer feedback on its starting performance in 2026, thanks in no small part to its head start over

news.title: FDA blocks publication of COVID, shingles vaccine safety studies: NYT
news.snippet: The FDA-conducted studies reportedly found COVID vaccines and GSK's shingles vaccine Shringrix safe and effective, according to The New York Times.

news.title: Bayer diagnoses how to build trust in cell and gene therapies
news.snippet: Cell and gene therapy developers must build continuity of engagement to create trust in the modalities, according to a report by Bayer’s venture capital wing.

news.title: Touting $529B in savings over 10 years, White House looks to expand MFN deals with pharma

news.title: Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly fined by French regulator over obesity drug promotions
news.snippet: As the promotion battle between old rivals Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly heats up, regulators in France are reminding the world’s two biggest obesity drugmakers what happens when promotion goes too far.

news.title: Lilly to invest $4.5B more into massive Indiana manufacturing complex
news.snippet: With commitments to invest more than $50 billion in building up its U.S. manufacturing presence, Eli Lilly has earmarked an additional $4.5 billion to upgrade two of its three planned production facil

news.title: After Alzheimer's agitation nod, Axsome jacks up Auvelity's peak sales projection to $8B
news.snippet: Fresh off gaining an FDA expansion for Auvelity as a treatment for agitation associated with Alzheimer’s disease, Axsome Therapeutics has projected peak sales for the non-antipsychotic drug to reach $

news.title: BioNTech to slash 1,860 jobs, exit sites in Germany and Singapore in major manufacturing pullback
news.snippet: The German vaccine maker plans to wind down operations at a handful of its sites in Germany plus one in Singapore, putting as many as 1,860 jobs on the chopping block.

news.title: Seaport’s IPO adventure, obesity pill battles, and Makary’s troubles
news.snippet: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: Seaport Therapeutics’ successful IPO, obesity pill battles, and the FDA commissioner's White House troubles.

news.title: STAT+: More political interference at the FDA?
news.snippet: A political appointee gets in the way of a Sanofi drug's approval, and more from STAT's D.C. Diagnosis newsletter.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Sanofi and an FDA voucher, FDA rethinking a rejection, and more
news.snippet: Sanofi asked the FDA to pull teplizumab out of a speedy review voucher program after a political appointee disagreed with staff over its approval

news.title: STAT+: FDA revisits a rare cancer treatment it rejected a few months ago
news.snippet: Secret drug pricing deals, a virtual AI cancer clinic, and other biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: FDA to reconsider treatment for rare cancer after its surprise rejection
news.snippet: The companies developing Ebvallo reached an agreement with the FDA to address its main reason for rejecting the drug.

news.title: Hospital shootings steadily increased since 2000
news.snippet: What the FDA has lost, why hantavirus isn't the next pandemic, and more health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Next-gen Duchenne drug from Entrada disappoints
news.snippet: A next-gen Duchenne therapy from Entrada Therapeutics fell short in an early study, as a number of companies race to make improved drugs.

news.title: STAT+: Color me skeptical: Drinking gold is not an ALS cure
news.snippet: In this week's Biotech Scorecard newsletter, Adam Feuerstein takes on the company touting gold microdust suspended in water as an ALS treatment.

news.title: STAT+: Angelini Pharma buys Catalyst Pharmaceuticals and its rare disease drugs for $4.1B
news.snippet: The Italian firm Angelini Pharma is buying Catalyst Pharmaceuticals, the maker of rare disease drugs, for $4.1 billion.

news.title: NHS cancer jab could save patients hours in hospital
news.snippet: Thousands of patients will be offered a new injectable form of an immunotherapy drug that takes minutes.

news.title: The struggle to get hold of medication in England is set to get worse
news.snippet: People living with conditions include heart problems, stroke risks, eye infections and bipolar are unable to get hold of the drugs they rely on.
